title: Funding the future of European biotech
snippet: In this episode of Denatured, you'll be hearing from Edoardo Negroni, co-founder & managing partner at AurorA-TT and Naveed Siddiqi, senior partner, Venture Investments at Novo Holdings. We debate whether Europe’s world-class science can be matched by a truly integrated venture ecosystem—and what it would take, in pra...

title: Trump nominates new CDC director, appoints various leaders to embattled agency
snippet: Erica Schwartz, a former deputy surgeon general and member of the U.S. military, will take over from what has amounted to a rapid succession of CDC leaders over the past year.

title: Drug pricing watchdog calls for increased transparency into FDA’s accelerated approval decisions
snippet: Greater transparency in FDA decision-making was possibly the most common theme for stakeholders interviewed by the Institute for Clinical and Economic Review in a new report focused on strengthening the agency’s accelerated approval program.

title: FDA mulls compounding for peptides previously flagged over safety risks
snippet: With Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. declaring himself “a big fan of peptides,” the FDA has arranged an advisory committee to discuss allowing compounders to make molecules despite earlier safety concerns.

title: MeiraGTx, spying hope in a failed trial, buys back eye disease gene therapy from J&J
snippet: J&J will hand over the rights to bota-vec for $25 million upfront, clearing MeiraGTx to seek regulatory approvals in the U.S. and EU in 2027.

title: Accelerating discovery of cancer causes for prevention in the era of rising early-onset cancers.
snippet: Cancer in younger adults is rising globally, with notable birth-cohort effects. This epidemiological shift underscores the urgent need to accelerate the identification of novel causes and underlying biological networks, with the aim of translating these insights into prevention and interception strategies. In this perspective, we revisit the major milestone...

title: A recipe for chaos: Extrachromosomal DNA and the hallmarks of cancer.
snippet: Some aggressive cancers exhibit a level of rapid genome change and therapy resistance that is difficult to explain. Research over the past decade has shown that extrachromosomal DNA (ecDNA) can be the cause. When oncogenic genetic elements untether from chromosomes and no longer follow Mendelian inheritance, genomic chaos and accelerated evolution ensue, ge...

title: The evolving role of OMICS in gastrointestinal tumor biology and clinical practice.
snippet: Due to late diagnosis, high molecular diversity, and limited response to therapeutic intervention, gastrointestinal (GI) cancers result in a considerable number of cancer-related deaths. Classic clinicopathological classification and single omics analysis fail to adequately convey the extensive biological complexity related to progression of disease, develo...

title: Clonal biases dictate availability of colonic cancer driver mutations for transformation.
snippet: Aged normal tissues harbour cancer mutations predisposing to transformation. However, how different pro-oncogenic events in the human colon compare in frequency, behaviour and subsequent transformation risk remains unclear. Here, we analyse mutation hotspot regions in five colorectal cancer genes (APC, KRAS, TP53, FBXW7 and CTNNB1) using targeted sequencing...

title: Single-cell and spatial transcriptomics reveal that the CXCL12-CXCR4 axis drives the immune-desert phenotype in small cell lung cancer by recruiting immunosuppressive CXCR4neutrophils and S100A8monocytes.
snippet: Small cell lung cancer (SCLC) is a recalcitrant malignancy with limited responses to immunotherapy, largely due to its uniquely immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment (TME). However, the molecular mechanisms driving this phenotype remain incompletely understood. We integrated single-cell RNA sequencing and Xenium in situ spatial transcriptomics to analyze...
